title: Database Schema Analysis
executiveSummary: The SnapBack database schema is well-structured with a focus on privacy-first design principles. It uses PostgreSQL with Drizzle ORM and includes tables for user management, API key tracking, code snapshots, telemetry, security events, and analytics. The schema implements proper foreign key relationships with CASCADE deletes for data integrity and includes partitioned tables for time-series data.
keyFeatures: Privacy-first design with metadata-only storage by default
Foreign key constraints with CASCADE deletes for data integrity
Partitioned tables for time-series data (error logs, feature usage, API usage)
Security event monitoring and rate limiting
Snapshot and file metadata storage with optional cloud backup

userManagement.description: User authentication and profile management based on Better Auth
userManagement.relations: Users have sessions, accounts, passkeys, and two-factor authentication records

organizationManagement.description: Team and organization structure with membership management
organizationManagement.relations: Organizations have members and invitations, with team structures

subscriptionBilling.description: Subscription and billing management with usage tracking
subscriptionBilling.relations: Subscriptions are linked to users or organizations with usage limits

apiKeySystem.description: API key management with permissions and usage tracking
apiKeySystem.relations: API keys are linked to users and organizations with usage tracking

codeSnapshots.description: Privacy-first code snapshot storage with metadata
codeSnapshots.relations: Snapshots contain multiple files with metadata, linked to users and API keys
codeSnapshots.privacyPrinciple: Store only metadata by default, file content requires explicit user opt-in

telemetryAnalytics.relations: Time-series data with daily aggregations for performance
telemetryAnalytics.partitioning: Feature usage and API usage logs are partitioned by month

securityMonitoring.description: Security event monitoring and violation tracking
securityMonitoring.relations: Security events track violations, bypasses, and rate limiting

errorLogging.description: Error logging with severity levels and partitioning
errorLogging.relations: Error logs are partitioned by month for performance

privacySecurityFeatures.dataEncryption: Server-side KMS encryption with Row Level Security (RLS) for snapshots
privacySecurityFeatures.privacyByDefault: Metadata-only storage for snapshots unless user explicitly opts in to cloud backup
privacySecurityFeatures.dataRetention: Configurable retention policies with retention_config table
privacySecurityFeatures.accessControl: API key permissions system with granular controls
privacySecurityFeatures.rateLimiting: Token bucket algorithm implementation for rate limiting

performanceOptimizations.indexing: Comprehensive indexing strategy with unique and composite indexes
performanceOptimizations.partitioning: Monthly partitioning for time-series data tables
performanceOptimizations.caching: Response cache table for API responses
performanceOptimizations.materializedViews: Planned materialized views for analytics performance

schemaQuality.normalization: Well-normalized schema with proper foreign key relationships
schemaQuality.constraints: Comprehensive use of constraints for data integrity
schemaQuality.documentation: Good inline documentation explaining privacy principles and design decisions
schemaQuality.naming: Consistent naming conventions following snake_case for columns

findings.description: Strong privacy-first design principles with clear documentation
findings.details: The schema implements privacy-by-default with metadata-only storage and explicit opt-in for sensitive data

findings.description: Comprehensive foreign key relationships with CASCADE deletes
findings.details: Proper data integrity with CASCADE deletes ensuring related data is cleaned up appropriately

findings.description: Partitioned tables for time-series data performance
findings.details: Monthly partitioning for error logs, feature usage, and API usage logs improves query performance
Rich telemetry data with daily aggregations and idempotency support

findings.description: Some tables may benefit from additional indexes for specific query patterns
findings.details: Depending on query patterns, additional composite indexes might improve performance

recommendations.description: Implement additional composite indexes based on common query patterns
recommendations.details: Analyze query logs to identify frequently used WHERE clauses and create appropriate composite indexes

recommendations.description: Consider adding audit trails for sensitive operations
recommendations.details: Implement audit logging for changes to user permissions, API keys, and subscription changes
Implement alerts for foreign key constraint violations and other data integrity issues
